title: six6 Memory Candidate
description: One JSONL record emitted by memory-candidate-generator before human review. review_id is a batch-local human label; candidate_id is the machine-stable routing and ingestion key; neither is reused as the final FalkorDB Memory.id.

review_id.description: Batch-local display ordinal for human review reports. It is serialized for auditability but must not be used for approval routing or database ingestion.

candidate_id.description: Candidate-layer stable identifier used for approval routing, discard routing, and idempotent ingestion lookup.

topic.description: Short title for the distilled memory candidate.

content.description: Distilled summary, not a raw source backup.

timestamp.description: Source event time when known; otherwise generation time.

maturity.description: Current V1 candidates normally use 1 unless a generator has a stronger maturity policy.

source.description: Logical source path or process name.

source_file.description: Physical source file retained for audit and routing.

source_section.description: Source heading or section name when available.

hash_input.description: Canonical audit string used to derive candidate_id.

$comment:
Idempotency rule: candidate_id = '<prefix>-<YYMMDD>-<sha256(source_file + "
" + source_section + "
" + topic + "
" + content)[0:12]>'. Prefix maps to category: fact=fac, protocol=pro, lesson=les, relation=rel, evolution=evo; use mem only for legacy/general candidates.
